reason: The text explicitly depicts the regular people (הַעָם) as active participants in decision-making. The people gather collectively (וַיִּקָּהֵל הָעָם), voice their demands directly to Aaron (וַיֹּאמְרוּ אֵלָיו), collectively decide to provide their gold earrings for the project (וַיִּתְפָּרְקוּ כָּל־הָעָם אֶת־נִזְמֵי הַזָּהָב), and collectively participate in the resulting religious practice (וַיִּשְׁתַּחֲווּ־לוֹ וַיִּזְבְּחוּ־לוֹ). Rather than being passive subjects, they initiate the demand, articulate their grievances about Moses, provide material support, and approve the outcome. While Aaron executes the specific plan (creating the calf), the people are clearly portrayed as driving the decision-making process through collective action and voice.
